item.dc:creator: Kyle Stewart
Jennifer Jett
item.description: House Republicans delayed a vote on a war powers resolution that would restrict President Donald Trump’s ability to continue U.S. military operations in Iran.

media:content.media:text: Iran-Music Ceremony On Ruins Of Destroyed Synagogue
media:content.media:description: An unidentified man places an Iranian national flag on the ruins of a synagogue in Tehran, Iran, on May 14, 2026.

item.title: House Republicans cancel vote curbing Trump on Iran

item.description: Ashley Etienne, Dan Merica and Sarah Chamberlain join the Meet the Press NOW roundtable as Democratic National Committee chair Ken Martin faces backlash after releasing the report on former Vice President Harris’ 2024 election loss. NBC News’ Natasha Korecki explains how the long-awaited report came after intense pressure from Democrats.

media:content.media:title: ‘A complete and total failure’: Calls grow for DNC chair Ken Martin to resign over 2024 autopsy
media:content.media:description: Ashley Etienne, Dan Merica and Sarah Chamberlain join the Meet the Press NOW roundtable as Democratic National Committee chair Ken Martin faces backlash after releasing the report on former Vice President Harris’ 2024 election loss. NBC News’ Natasha Korecki explains how the long-awaited report came after intense pressure from Democrats.

item.title: ‘A complete and total failure’: Calls grow for DNC chair Ken Martin to resign over 2024 autopsy

item.description: Watch highlights of the star-studded sendoff as Stephen Colbert brings the curtain down on 33 years of “The Late Show,” with legends like Jon Stewart, Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and many surprises.

media:content.media:title: Watch highlights from Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' finale
media:content.media:description: Watch highlights of the star-studded sendoff as Stephen Colbert brings the curtain down on 33 years of “The Late Show,” with legends like Jon Stewart, Paul McCartney, Bryan Cranston, and many surprises.

item.title: Watch highlights from Stephen Colbert's 'Late Show' finale

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: British police urged witnesses to come forward on Friday as they continued to investigate potential offenses by former Prince Andrew related to Jeffrey Epstein.

media:content.media:text: King Charles III And Queen Camilla Attend The 2025 Easter Service At St George's Chapel
media:content.media:description: Prince Andrew, Duke of York attends the traditional Easter Sunday Mattins Service at St George's Chapel, Windsor Castle on April 20, 2025 in Windsor, England.

media:content.media:text: Virginia Giuffre (then Roberts) with Prince Andrew and Ghislaine Maxwell at Prince Andrew's London home in a photo released with court documents.
media:content.media:description: Virginia Giuffre (then Roberts) with Prince Andrew and Ghislaine Maxwell.

media:content.media:text: Jeffrey Epstein.
media:content.media:description: An undated photo of Jeffrey Epstein released by the Department of Justice in 2025.

media:content.media:text: The State Funeral Of Queen Elizabeth II
media:content.media:description: Britain's King Charles III and Former Prince Andrew in London in 2022.

item.title: Police urge witnesses come forward in Prince Andrew investigation

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: An independent investigation into the nation’s largest public pension, the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, has been completed.

media:content.media:text: USA News - 20 Jun 2022
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Margaret Brown.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Rich Wiggins.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

item.title: Nation’s largest public pension fund plagued by secrecy and underperformance, probe finds

item.description: GOP senators abandoned a planned vote on a proposed $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that would compensate Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

media:content.media:title: Trump faces GOP resistance on compensation fund, ballroom money
media:content.media:description: GOP senators abandoned a planned vote on a proposed $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund that would compensate Trump allies who say they were unfairly prosecuted by the Biden administration. NBC News’ Garrett Haake reports.

item.title: Trump faces GOP resistance on compensation fund, ballroom money

item.description: He was among the most winning drivers in NASCAR history and now the sudden death of Kyle Busch has left the racing world stunned. NBC News’ Sam Brock reports on what both NASCAR and Busch’s family have revealed about his death.

media:content.media:title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dead at age 41
media:content.media:description: He was among the most winning drivers in NASCAR history and now the sudden death of Kyle Busch has left the racing world stunned. NBC News’ Sam Brock reports on what both NASCAR and Busch’s family have revealed about his death.

item.title: NASCAR star Kyle Busch dead at age 41

item.dc:creator: Kaitlin Sullivan
item.description: GLP-1 drugs may be linked to a lower risk of cancer progression, according to new research that will be presented next week at the American Society of Clinical Oncology’s annual meeting.

media:content.media:text: Lung cancer MRI.
media:content.media:description: Lung cancer seen on an MRI scan.

item.title: GLP-1 drugs may reduce the risk of cancer progressing, study suggests

item.dc:creator: Harry Rabinowitz
item.description: Shop the best Memorial Day deals on mattresses, home appliances, beauty, tech and more from brands like Brooklinen, Ninja, Levoit, Supergoop and Apple.

media:content.media:credit: Amazon; Kara Birnbaum / NBC News; Brooklinen
media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

media:content.media:text: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.
media:content.media:description: It may not be Black Friday, but Memorial Day weekend is one of the best times to shop before summer hits.

item.title: The 93+ absolute best Memorial Day sales worth shopping

item.description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

media:content.media:title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight
media:content.media:description: Watch live coverage as SpaceX launches the twelfth test flight of Starship. The company claims the third-generation prototype, dubbed V3, is larger, more powerful and a step closer to being fully reusable. The rocket is scheduled to liftoff from SpaceX's StarBase in Brownsville, Texas.

item.title: SpaceX launches prototype of Starship rocket in crucial test flight

item.dc:creator: Chantal Da Silva
item.description: British police urged witnesses to come forward on Friday as they continued to investigate potential offenses by former Prince Andrew related to Jeffrey Epstein.

media:content.media:text: King Charles III And Queen Camilla Attend The 2025 Easter Service At St George's Chapel

item.title: Police urge witnesses come forward in Prince Andrew investigation

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
item.description: A 12th hantavirus case has been confirmed after a crew member aboard the MV Hondius cruise ship, which arrived in the Netherlands last week for disinfection, tested positive, the World Health Organization said Friday.

media:content.media:text: MV Hondius Cruise Ship In Quarantine In The Netherlands
media:content.media:description: The MV Hondius docked in Rotterdam, Netherlands on May 20, 2026.

item.title: Hantavirus cases linked to cruise ship rise to 12 after crew member tests positive

item.dc:creator: Andrew Greif
item.description: Competing while using banned substances is one of elite sports’ most fiercely protected red lines.

media:content.media:text: Fred Kerley competes in the men's 100m during the IAAF Wanda Diamond League: Golden Gala Pietro Mennea in Rome, Italy, in June 2025.
media:content.media:description: Fred Kerley competes in the men's 100 meters during the IAAF Wanda Diamond League: Golden Gala Pietro Mennea in Rome in June.

media:content.media:text: Shane Ryan of Team Ireland during a training session at the 2024 Summer Olympic Games in Paris, France in July 2024.
media:content.media:description: Fred Kerley competes in the men's 100 meters during the IAAF Wanda Diamond League: Golden Gala Pietro Mennea in Rome in June.

item.title: A new sporting event’s controversial pitch: Performance-enhancing drugs welcome

item.dc:creator: Gretchen Morgenson
item.description: An independent investigation into the nation’s largest public pension, the California Public Employees’ Retirement System, has been completed.

media:content.media:text: USA News - 20 Jun 2022
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Margaret Brown.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

media:content.media:text: Rich Wiggins.
media:content.media:description: The headquarters of the California Public Employees' Retirement System, or CalPERS, in Sacramento.

item.title: Nation’s largest public pension fund plagued by secrecy and underperformance, probe finds

item.dc:creator: Kyle Stewart
Jennifer Jett
item.description: House Republicans delayed a vote on a war powers resolution that would restrict President Donald Trump’s ability to continue U.S. military operations in Iran.

media:content.media:text: Iran-Music Ceremony On Ruins Of Destroyed Synagogue
media:content.media:description: An unidentified man places an Iranian national flag on the ruins of a synagogue in Tehran, Iran, on May 14, 2026.

item.title: House Republicans cancel vote curbing Trump on Iran

item.dc:creator: Claire Cardona
item.description: The family of one of the gunmen in a deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque this week said his exposure to hateful and extremist content online “contributed to his descent into radicalized ideologies and violent beliefs.”The Vazquez family apologized for the actions Caleb Vazquez is accused of, which they said there was no excuse for, and spoke out against online spaces that “normalize hatred” in a statement provided to NBC San Diego by the family’s attorney

media:content.media:text: People take part in an early morning call to prayer at the Islamic Center of San Diego
media:content.media:description: The Islamic Center of San Diego reopened Wednesday following a shooting Monday that killed three people.

item.title: Family says suspect in San Diego mosque shooting was influenced by hateful content

item.dc:creator: Natasha Korecki
Ben Kamisar
item.description: The Democratic National Committee plunged into a fresh round of chaos after Chair Ken Martin was forced to release an autopsy report he commissioned about the failed 2024 presidential campaign.

media:content.media:text: Ken Martin is partially obscured by a heavily-blurred foreground.
media:content.media:description: Ken Martin, chairman of the Democratic National Committee, following a press conference with Texas Democrats at the International Union of Painters and Allied Trades union hall in Aurora, Ill. on Aug. 5, 2025.

item.title: Pressure on DNC Chair Ken Martin builds amid questions over how he handled the 2024 autopsy

item.dc:creator: Gabe Gutierrez
Raquel Coronell Uribe
item.description: The president said that due to the Iran war and “other things,” he wasn’t sure if he’d be attending the nuptials of Donald Trump Jr. and Bettina Anderson this weekend.

media:content.media:text: Donald Trump Jr., and Bettina Anderson arrive at Andrews Air Force Base, Md., on July 29, 2025.
media:content.media:description: Donald Trump Jr., and Bettina Anderson arrive at Andrews Air Force Base, Md., on July 29, 2025.

item.title: Trump says he might skip Donald Jr.’s wedding because of Iran

item.dc:creator: Ryan J. Reilly
Katherine Doyle
Frank Thorp V
item.description: Applications are already rolling into the Justice Department from hopefuls aiming for some of the nearly $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund, even though the process can’t officially begin until commissioners are chosen to decide how the money is doled out.

media:content.media:text: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche Meets With Lawmakers On Capitol Hill Over President Trump's 1.8 Billion Compensation Fund
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche arrives Thursday at the Capitol, where was expected to meet with Republican members of Congress to address concerns about the newly announced "anti-weaponization" fund.

media:content.media:text: Sen. Thom Tillis, R-N.C., on Capitol Hill on April 14.
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche arrives Thursday at the Capitol, where was expected to meet with Republican members of Congress to address concerns about the newly announced "anti-weaponization" fund.

media:content.media:text: Former Trump campaign official Michael Caputo arrives on Capitol Hill in Washington
media:content.media:description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche arrives Thursday at the Capitol, where was expected to meet with Republican members of Congress to address concerns about the newly announced "anti-weaponization" fund.

item.title: Trump’s $1.8B fund isn’t officially open yet. That hasn’t stopped applications.

item.dc:creator: Claire Cardona
item.description: The family of one of the gunmen in a deadly shooting at a San Diego mosque this week said his exposure to hateful and extremist content online “contributed to his descent into radicalized ideologies and violent beliefs.”The Vazquez family apologized for the actions Caleb Vazquez is accused of, which they said there was no excuse for, and spoke out against online spaces that “normalize hatred” in a statement provided to NBC San Diego by the family’s attorney

media:content.media:text: People take part in an early morning call to prayer at the Islamic Center of San Diego
media:content.media:description: The Islamic Center of San Diego reopened Wednesday following a shooting Monday that killed three people.

item.title: Family says suspect in San Diego mosque shooting was influenced by hateful content

item.dc:creator: Corky Siemaszko
item.description: A white supremacist group faces a federal discrimination lawsuit filed by a real estate broker who says she was barred from purchasing property in their rural Arkansas community because of her Jewish ancestry, Black husband and biracial children

media:content.media:text: Eric Orwoll, left, and Scott Thomas in Ravenden, Ark., in 2025.
media:content.media:description: Eric Orwoll, left, and Scott Thomas at the Return to the Land settlement in Ravenden, Ark., in November. Orwoll and Thomas are among the defendants named in a discrimination suit.

media:content.media:text: Eric Orwoll at his "whites-only" settlement in Ravenden, Ark., in 2025.
media:content.media:description: Eric Orwoll, left, and Scott Thomas at the Return to the Land settlement in Ravenden, Ark., in November. Orwoll and Thomas are among the defendants named in a discrimination suit.

item.title: Suit targets whites-only Arkansas community over applicant’s rejection

item.dc:creator: Jason Kane
Sara G. Miller
Erin McLaughlin
item.description: A contractor that administers the military’s healthcare benefits to more than 4 million people issued a broad apology to its beneficiaries this week for problems with its coverage

media:content.media:text: FORT WORTH, TEXAS - FEBRUARY 11, 2026: Guy Shoemaker, 64, poses for a portrait outside his home in Fort Worth, Texas, on Wednesday, Feb. 11, 2026. After his TRICARE plan transitioned to TriWest Healthcare Alliance, tens of thousands of dollars in previously approved claims for life-saving treatment and scans for his throat cancer were pulled back or denied because of a data error.
media:content.media:description: FORT WORTH, TEXAS - FEBRUARY 11, 2026: Guy Shoemaker, 64, poses for a portrait outside his home in Fort Worth, Texas, on Wednesday, Feb. 11, 2026. After his TRICARE plan transitioned to TriWest Healthcare Alliance, tens of thousands of dollars in previously approved claims for life-saving treatment and scans for his throat cancer were pulled back or denied because of a data error.

item.title: Military healthcare contractor apologizes to 4 million beneficiaries

item.dc:creator: Minyvonne Burke
Kathryn Prociv
item.description: Memorial Day weekend travelers face a wet and potentially disruptive start to the holiday, with storms expected to bring heavy rain, flash flooding and travel delays across large parts of the country.

media:content.media:text: Rangers Rockies Baseball
media:content.media:description: Fans don raining gear as a storm passes over Coors Field in the fourth inning of a baseball game between the Texas Rangers and Colorado Rockies, Wednesday, May 20, 2026, in Denver.

media:content.media:text: Thunderstorm Passes Through New York City
media:content.media:description: Storm clouds begin to move over the skyline of midtown Manhattan and the Empire State Building in New York City on Wednesday.

item.title: Memorial Day travel snarled as storms threaten flooding from Texas to Northeast

item.dc:creator: Nicole Acevedo
Will Ujek
item.description: The Justice Department said the defendants defrauded Minnesota Medicaid and other programs of more than $90 million, and two of them billed for autism services that were never provided.

media:content.media:text: Colin McDonald gestures with both hands while speaking at a lectern. RFK Jr. is visible behind him.
media:content.media:description: Assistant Attorney General for the National Fraud Enforcement Division Colin McDonald addresses media to announce actions for combating fraud in Minnesota at the U.S. Attorney's Office for the District of Minnesota in Minneapolis, on May 21, 2026.

item.title: Minnesota autism, disabled services providers among 15 charged with Medicaid fraud

item.dc:creator: Daniel Arkin
Allan Smith
item.description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani plans to launch a livestream series called “Talk with the People”

media:content.media:text: New York Mayor-elect Zohran Mamdani at his transition office
media:content.media:description: New York Mayor Zohran Mamdani is launching a livestream series on Twitch.

item.title: NYC Mayor Zohran Mamdani is launching a chat show on Twitch
